title: ReflectiChain: Epistemic Grounding in LLM-Driven World Models for Supply Chain Resilience
summary: This research tackles a key problem in supply chain management: AI systems that understand written policies but can't handle real-world physics, while systems that optimize logistics can't understand complex constraints written in natural language. The solution combines language models with a special computer simulation that tracks how goods flow through networks while keeping everything physically realistic, using a learning method that separately handles different types of uncertainty.
key_points: The system bridges two AI worlds—language understanding and logical optimization—that normally don't work well together
It uses a compact mathematical representation of supply networks that respects real-world physical limits like conservation of materials
When tested on semiconductor supply disruptions, it performed 33% better at explaining its reasoning, kept functioning 82% of the time during attacks, and actually got better under moderate stress
why_it_matters: Supply chains are critical to everything we buy, and knowing when and how they'll break down during crises could help companies survive disruptions while preventing widespread shortages.
